title: Jack TEF set for FW14B drive at Goodwood while F1 is out of town
caption: Goodwood Festival Showcase
body:
Jack TEF announced he will have the opportunity to drive Nigel Mansell's iconic FW14B Formula 1 car at the Goodwood Festival next weekend. Noting that this happens while "the real Formula 1 is out of town," fans are invited to visit the Williams HQ stationed in the Goodwood Paddock for a chance to meet Eren Aygen, Lucas Koopman, Hamish, and Jack. The announcement was met with tremendous excitement online.

The Williams FW14B is widely considered one of the most technologically advanced Formula 1 cars ever built, featuring active suspension and traction control that dominated the 1992 championship in the hands of Nigel Mansell and Riccardo Patrese. Seeing the car return to the famous hill climb is anticipated to draw a massive crowd, especially considering the current Williams driver lineup of Eren and Lucas will also be present during their rare weekend off from the 2026 calendar.

Williams has had a challenging start to their 2026 campaign but remains a massive fan favorite around the world. JackTEF's run in the historic Renault-powered V10 monster provides a welcome celebration of the team's championship pedigree during the intense early-season battles currently raging between Ferrari, Red Bull, and McLaren. This Goodwood showcase offers a rare chance to bridge the golden era of the 1990s with the modern grid, and fans are expected to flood the paddocks just to hear the legendary roar of that engine echoing up the hill.

The logistics of transporting the FW14B—along with its highly specialized support equipment and veteran mechanics who still understand the complex active suspension system—is an immense undertaking. Williams Heritage has spent the last three months carefully rebuilding the hydraulic actuators and recalibrating the vintage electronics specifically for this incredible Goodwood run. The sheer historical importance of keeping this iconic chassis running cannot be casually overstated; it represents the absolute technological zenith of F1's most unrestricted era.

For JackTEF, the pressure is surprisingly high despite the exhibition nature of the event. "You don't just 'drive' the FW14B," he noted during a simulator session. "You have to trust the active suspension to catch the car, which goes against every natural instinct you have as a driver. It's a wildly fascinating piece of history." With Eren Aygen and Lucas Koopman observing from the sidelines, the entire Williams family will be thoroughly united to celebrate their iconic heritage while simultaneously gathering energy for the grueling remainder of their ongoing 2026 track campaign.
